summary: PR 2645 linked `melee/gr/grinishie2.c` by changing it from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py` and adjusted GALE01 split/symbol metadata for its data, sdata, sbss, and sdata2 ranges. The bot report shows linked code increased by 5176 bytes, matched data increased by 538 bytes, linked data increased by 570 bytes, and two new matches for `main/melee/gr/grinishie2` `.sbss` and `.data`.

key_files.role: Changed `melee/gr/grinishie2.c` object status from NonMatching to Matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunk around MatchingFor, Object(NonMatching, "melee/gr/grinishie2.c") -> Object(Matching, "melee/gr/grinishie2.c")

key_files.role: Adjusted `melee/gr/grinishie2.c` `.data` split end from `0x803E4C78` to `0x803E4C72` while leaving text/sdata/sbss/sdata2 ranges shown in the excerpt.
Updated symbol metadata around `grI2` data ownership: changed several globals to local labels, resized objects, added local string/float labels, and reduced `grI2_804D6A00` size.

what_changed.text: The `grinishie2.c` `.data` split end was narrowed from `0x803E4C78` to `0x803E4C72`.

Several `grI2` symbol entries were reclassified or resized, including adding `...data.0` at `0x803E4A60`, making string labels at `0x803E4BF4`, `0x803E4C34`, `0x803E4C58`, and `0x803E4C68` local, resizing `grI2_803E4C00` from `0x78` to `0x34`, reducing `grI2_804D6A00` from size `0x8` to `0x4`, and changing `grI2_804DB610`-`grI2_804DB61C` sdata2 floats to local labels.
The decomp bot reported two new matches for `main/melee/gr/grinishie2`: `.sbss` improved from 50.00% to 100.00% and `.data` improved from 99.44% to 100.00%.

smart_moves.text: The PR kept the source-code linking change small and paired it with the specific split and symbol metadata adjustments needed for `grinishie2` data matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: only config/GALE01/splits.txt, config/GALE01/symbols.txt, and configure.py changed

smart_moves.text: The symbol edits used local labels for multiple strings/floats instead of broad global `grI2_*` names where the diff indicates local ownership.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When linking a previously nonmatching object, verify and adjust section metadata in both `configure.py` and GALE01 split/symbol files; data ownership and local-label boundaries may need correction for the object to report cleanly matched.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py changes grinishie2 to Matching
raw_diff: splits.txt and symbols.txt metadata edits for grinishie2
text_corpus: bot report shows new `.data` and `.sbss` matches for main/melee/gr/grinishie2

naming_conventions.text: For data without semantic names in this slice, local labels such as `@185`, `@207`, `@208`, `@343`, `@344`, and `@345` were used instead of keeping or inventing global `grI2_*` names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt local label replacements and additions

naming_conventions.text: The existing stage/object prefix `grI2_` was retained for remaining global objects such as `grI2_803E4A60`, `grI2_803E4AB4`, `grI2_803E4C00`, and `grI2_804D6A00`.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: This PR used metadata-only matching tactics: object status promotion plus split-boundary, symbol-size, symbol-scope, and local-label corrections. No C source or assembly tactic is visible in the slice.
The data split boundary was tightened by 6 bytes (`0x803E4C78` to `0x803E4C72`), matching the bot report's total data delta of -6 bytes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report `Total data: 1211228 bytes (-6 bytes)`

notes: PR body is empty and there are no review comments or reviews in the loaded slice.
The slice contains no C source changes, so lessons are limited to object linking, split/symbol metadata, and reported matching effects.
The bot report supports new `.data` and `.sbss` matches, but no local build command, objdiff invocation, or human review rationale is present in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Object linking can require coordinated configure, split, and symbol metadata updates
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2645 linked `melee/gr/grinishie2.c` by switching it to `Object(Matching, ...)` and updating GALE01 split/symbol metadata for data sections; the bot reported new `.data` and `.sbss` matches for `main/melee/gr/grinishie2`.

source_update_candidates.title: `melee/gr/grinishie2.c` linked as Matching in PR 2645
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2645 changed `melee/gr/grinishie2.c` from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`, with corresponding GALE01 split and symbol metadata updates. The bot report listed two new matches for `main/melee/gr/grinishie2`: `.sbss` 50.00% to 100.00% and `.data` 99.44% to 100.00%.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a path-specific historical fact about the object becoming linked/matching, suitable for curator review before adding to path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Check grinishie2 data ownership and local labels from PR 2645
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2645 changed several `grI2` data/sdata/sdata2 entries from global `grI2_*` names to local labels and resized `grI2_803E4C00` and `grI2_804D6A00`; curator may want to reflect or verify these ownership boundaries in data records.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides concrete symbol metadata edits, but intake should not promote them into a source corpus without curator validation.

rejection_notes: Do not infer reviewer intent; the loaded slice has no review comments.
Do not generalize that all `grI2_*` symbols should become local labels; only the addresses shown in the diff are supported.
Do not claim C source-level cleanup or assembly reconstruction tactics; no source file changes are present.
